kits.label: LSK114
kits.summary: Ligation sequencing for longer fragments and higher-yield single-sample DNA workflows.

consequences.route_implication: Best when read length and consensus quality matter more than speed.

notes: Best fit for high-molecular-weight DNA and consensus-focused workflows.
A sensible default when no barcoding is needed.

kits.label: RBK114.24
kits.summary: Rapid barcoding for multiplexed DNA runs with fast library preparation.

consequences.route_implication: Biases the setup toward pooled sample handling and per-barcode separation.

notes: Strong fit for rapid multiplexed DNA workflows.
Use when turnaround matters more than maximum fragment preservation.

kits.label: NBD114.24
kits.summary: Native barcoding for multiplexed DNA runs where read quality matters more than prep speed.

consequences.typical_use: Multiplexed DNA with stronger emphasis on read quality.
consequences.route_implication: Keeps the setup barcode-oriented while staying closer to ligation-style quality.

notes: Sensible when you want multiplexing without giving up too much read quality.

kits.label: Rapid RNA or cDNA workflow
kits.summary: RNA-derived workflow that behaves more like DNA library handling than native direct RNA.

consequences.barcode_trimming: Depends on the exact barcoding strategy.
consequences.typical_use: RNA virome routes that are cDNA-based rather than direct RNA.
consequences.route_implication: Keeps the route RNA-focused without forcing a native-RNA-only assumption.

notes: Useful when RNA quality or throughput constraints make cDNA workflows more practical than native direct RNA.

kits.label: Direct RNA
kits.summary: Native RNA sequencing that preserves strand and modification information at the cost of stricter input requirements.

consequences.typical_use: Native RNA when transcript structure or modifications matter.
consequences.route_implication: Pushes the selector toward RNA-specific flow cells and more cautious throughput expectations.

notes: Best when native RNA features matter and the input RNA quality is high enough.

kits.label: Amplicon workflow
kits.summary: Standard ONT-style amplicon route for shorter targeted products such as 12S.

consequences.barcode_trimming: Primer or adapter cleanup is usually still relevant.
consequences.route_implication: Steers the setup toward lighter throughput and amplicon-aware downstream interpretation.

notes: This is the cleanest default for 12S-like ONT amplicon work.

kits.label: Rapid PCR amplicon
kits.summary: PCR-based amplicon route for low-input targeted material where speed or input scarcity dominates.

consequences.typical_use: Low-input or fast targeted amplicon workflows.
consequences.route_implication: Adds more caution around amplification bias and shorter effective read structure.

notes: Best treated as an amplicon adaptation rather than a drop-in replacement for shotgun workflows.

kits.label: Barcoded metagenome
kits.summary: Multiplexed metagenomic handling rather than one-isolate-per-barcode handling.

consequences.typical_use: Pooled metagenomic samples where host association or broad profiling is still the goal.
consequences.route_implication: Keeps the setup metagenomic and pooled rather than isolate-centric.

notes: Use this when the dataset is complex metagenomic material rather than per-barcode isolates.

kits.label: Adaptive sampling
kits.summary: Real-time enrichment or depletion during sequencing rather than a passive control run.

consequences.demultiplexing: Only if barcodes were also introduced.
consequences.route_implication: Frames the analysis around enrichment efficiency and matched-control interpretation.

notes: Best interpreted together with a matched control when possible.

kits.label: Standard control
kits.summary: Matched non-adaptive comparison route for food-safety target recovery.

consequences.route_implication: Keeps the interpretation anchored to the non-adaptive baseline.

notes: Use this when the key question is comparison against adaptive sampling.

flow_cells.label: MinION R10.4.1
flow_cells.summary: Current general-purpose ONT DNA route for most microbial genomics and moderate-depth metagenomics.
notes: Good default for most new DNA runs.
Pairs well with Kit 14-era workflows and HAC or SUP basecalling.

flow_cells.label: PromethION R10.4.1
flow_cells.summary: High-output route for deeper metagenomics or larger cohorts.
notes: Strong fit for deep environmental metagenomics and large study batches.
Usually overkill for small pilot runs.

flow_cells.label: RNA flow cell
flow_cells.summary: RNA-specific route required for native direct RNA sequencing.
notes: Use this only for native direct RNA workflows.

basecalling_profiles.label: FAST for real-time monitoring
basecalling_profiles.summary: Use FAST when rapid monitoring matters more than maximum per-read accuracy.
notes: Good for live run assessment, field-style monitoring, or quick taxonomic triage.

basecalling_profiles.label: Balanced HAC
basecalling_profiles.summary: Use HAC as the standard production default when you want a balance of throughput and read accuracy.
notes: Best general-purpose default for many microbial genomics and metagenomics workflows.

basecalling_profiles.label: Maximum-accuracy SUP
basecalling_profiles.summary: Use SUP when assembly, AMR interpretation, or high-confidence consensus matters most.
notes: Best fit for assembly-heavy routes, isolate genomics, and high-stringency consensus work.

basecalling_profiles.label: Modified-base calling
basecalling_profiles.summary: Keep modification-aware basecalling in scope when methylation or modified bases matter biologically.
notes: Use only when the downstream question really depends on modification-aware interpretation.

basecalling_profiles.label: Already basecalled
basecalling_profiles.summary: Skip upstream basecalling guidance and start from the documented input state.
notes: Best when FASTQ or BAM already exists and you mainly need route-specific downstream guidance.

route_defaults.result_note: Default to R10.4.1 with rapid barcoding and HAC for broad environmental profiling.
Assembly- and AMR-heavy environmental DNA routes default to rapid barcoding with SUP accuracy.
Environmental RNA virome routes default to a cDNA-style RNA workflow unless native direct RNA is explicitly selected.
Targeted 12S routes default to an amplicon-first ONT setup rather than a shotgun one.
Single-isolate genomics defaults to a ligation-based DNA route with SUP-style consensus emphasis.
Barcoded isolates default to native barcoding with stronger emphasis on per-barcode quality and downstream comparison.
Clinical metagenome host-association routes stay metagenomic and pooled rather than isolate-centric, but matrix-specific guide notes still matter for plasma, BAL, and CSF.
Food-safety target-recovery defaults to adaptive sampling plus a high-accuracy consensus framing, with enrichment and matched-control interpretation kept visible.
